# Golden Examples Test Corpus
# These are known-good examples for regression testing

# Example 1: Basic t-test with all info
t(28) = 2.21, p = .035, d = 0.80, 95% CI [0.12, 1.48], N = 30, n1 = 15, n2 = 15

# Example 2: Correlation with CI
r(198) = .34, p < .001, 95% CI [.21, .45]

# Example 3: Chi-square
χ²(1) = 7.2, N = 120, phi = .10

# Example 4: F-test / ANOVA
F(2, 27) = 4.56, p < .05, η² = 0.25, ηp² = 0.30

# Example 5: Paired t-test
A paired-samples comparison yielded t(39) = 3.0, p = .004, d = 0.45.

# Example 6: Mixed decimal styles (stress test)
t(28) = 2,21, d = 0,45, CI [0,12; 0,78]

# Example 7: CI without level
d = 0.80, CI [0.12, 1.48]

# Example 8: Multiple stats per sentence
t(28) = 2.21, p = .035, d = 0.80, 95% CI [0.12, 1.48]. Another test: F(2, 27) = 4.56.

# Example 9: z-test
z = 2.33, p < .05

# Example 10: Regression context
The regression model was significant, F(3, 96) = 8.45, p < .001, R² = 0.21.

# Example 11: Edge case - df but no N
t(28) = 2.21, d = 0.80

# Example 12: CI before effect
95% CI [0.12, 1.48], d = 0.80, t(28) = 2.21

# Example 13: Negative values
t(28) = -2.21, CI [-0.3, 1.2]

# Example 14: Leading zeros
d = .45, CI [.12, .78]

# Example 15: Unicode minus
t(28) = −2.21, d = 0.80
